name: DGP braneworld
summary: Modifies gravity in the infrared rather than the UV: 4D gravity is induced on a 3-brane embedded in a 5D bulk with infinite extra dimension. Gravity transitions from 4D at sub-crossover scales r << r_c to 5D at super-crossover scales r >> r_c, with r_c ~ Hubble radius. Late-time cosmic acceleration arises geometrically from the 5D leakage rather than from a vacuum-energy term.

addresses_puzzles.note: Self-accelerating branch reproduces late-time acceleration without Lambda but is plagued by ghost instabilities and is disfavored by structure-growth data.

touches_benchmarks.note: DGP predicts c_T = c at all scales; not killed by GW170817 (unlike many Horndeski models).
Modified-gravity growth of structure deviates from LambdaCDM; DESI/EUCLID data constrain f sigma_8 in DGP.
Recovers GR in solar-system tests via Vainshtein screening below r_V.

introduces.note: 5D Planck mass + 4D Planck mass ratio sets r_c.

history: Dvali-Gabadadze-Porrati (2000). Self-accelerating branch popular as a Lambda alternative through 2005-2010; ghost identification and structure-growth disfavored it. Generalised Galileon descendents inherit some features.

references.citation: Dvali, Gabadadze, Porrati, '4D gravity on a brane in 5D Minkowski space', Phys. Lett. B 485, 208 (2000)
